texts.content:
Local residents rallied at City Hall on Monday to protest the proposed demolition of the historic Grand Theater. Built in 1928, the theater has hosted countless performances and community events over the decades. The city council voted 5–3 last week to approve plans for a commercial development on the site.

Supporters of preservation argue that the building contributes to the neighborhood's cultural identity and attracts tourism. Developers, however, claim the structure no longer meets safety codes and would require costly renovations.

questions.stem: What is the main issue described in the article?
options: A. The high cost of renovating the Grand Theater
B. The conflict over the future of a historic building
C. The outcome of the mayoral election
D. The decline of tourism in the area
questions.explanation: The article describes the dispute between preservation supporters and developers over the theater.

questions.stem: The word 'preservation' in paragraph 2 is closest in meaning to:
options: A. observation
B. conservation
C. decoration
D. reconstruction
questions.explanation: 'Preservation' here means protecting and maintaining the building in its original state.

questions.stem: Which of the following is an argument made by those who want to demolish the theater?
options: A. The theater no longer attracts audiences
B. The building is not safe enough by current standards
C. The location is not convenient for public transport
D. The property taxes on the building are too high
questions.explanation: Developers claim the structure 'no longer meets safety codes.'

texts.content:
Notice to All Tenants – Building Maintenance

Water will be shut off on Thursday, June 8, from 9:00 AM to 2:00 PM for pipe repairs in the basement. Please plan accordingly.

Additionally, the elevator will be out of service on June 10–11 for scheduled inspection. We apologize for any inconvenience.

For emergencies, contact the building superintendent at 555-0191.

options: A. The water will be turned off all day
B. The elevator is undergoing inspection
C. The basement will be cleaned
D. Emergency repairs will be performed
questions.explanation: The notice says the elevator will be out of service on June 10–11 for scheduled inspection.

questions.stem: What can be inferred about the relationship between the notice writer and the tenants?
options: A. The writer is a government official
B. The writer manages the building
C. The writer is a fellow tenant
D. The writer is an external contractor
questions.explanation: The notice is written from a building management perspective, announcing maintenance work and providing contact info for the superintendent.
